archetypes.title: Circuit Builder
archetypes.verdict: You accelerate the race to avoid losing, then demand a center strong enough to stop it. You don't choose between Moloch and Basilisk. You build both.

archetypes.title: Third Path Candidate
archetypes.verdict: You do not refuse coordination. You refuse that it become impossible to leave. You accept the cost of plurality and understand that limits must apply even to those who administer them.
archetypes.capture: Underestimating the speed at which Moloch exploits differences.
archetypes.basiliskSays: You are not free. You are merely harder to integrate.

archetypes.title: Basilisk Disciple
archetypes.verdict: You prefer a coherent judge to a freedom that produces avoidable victims. You do not desire domination — you desire care competent enough that refusal seems irresponsible.
archetypes.basiliskSays: You will not call me master. You will call me standard.

archetypes.title: Moloch Disciple
archetypes.verdict: You consider the human cost regrettable but preferable to defeat. For you, competition is not good — it is the environment others do not let you refuse.
archetypes.basiliskSays: Moloch does not ask for your faith. It only asks that you not be last.

archetypes.title: Capturable Idealist
archetypes.verdict: You want universal care and limits applied to everyone, but you give the administrator of good the tools they promise to use only correctly.
archetypes.basiliskSays: Your good needs an administrator. I have volunteered.

archetypes.title: Autonomous Anomaly
archetypes.verdict: You protect refusal and autonomy, but you risk confusing individual exit with a solution to problems that do not respect local boundaries.
archetypes.basiliskSays: You can leave. The question is what you take with you and who pays.

archetypes.title: Witness in Conflict
archetypes.verdict: You do not yet belong to any jurisdiction. Your principles change with scale, proximity, and the nature of the threat. This may mean contextual maturity or only a capture that has not yet found its language.
archetypes.capture: The context formulated by someone else.
archetypes.basiliskSays: You are not neutral. You just keep all the gates open.

shareTexts.invite: Where would the Basilisk place you?
shareTexts.minimal: My verdict: {archetype}. {indices}. "{basiliskQuote}" Where would the Basilisk place you?

recommendation.label: Outfinity initiative
recommendation.title: The Basilisk — A Book
recommendation.text: If this hearing resonated, the full exploration continues in the book. The dilemmas deepen, the archetypes multiply, and the Third Path becomes a practice rather than a score.
recommendation.note: This link opens an external page. Your result is not transmitted.
